title: feijoa sellowiana
intro: Feijoa sellowiana, also known as the guavasteen or pineapple guava, is a subtropical tree or shrub native to South America. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, with its leaves and fruits containing compounds that exhibit antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Feijoa sellowiana include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of Feijoa sellowiana, such as teas and infusions, can be made by steeping the leaves or fruits in hot water, or by using the plant's extracts in tinctures and capsules.
intro_study: According to "Anticancer research", feijoa sellowiana has antibacterial and antifungal properties, as well as MDR-reversal activity, indicating its potential as an antimicrobial and MDR-modulating agent.
